1.सारांशः: This is the invocation verse (मङ्गलश्लोक) at the beginning of Vadavali, where Sri Jayatirtha offers reverence to his guru and the divine.
1.प्रयोजनम्: To invoke divine blessings and set the auspicious tone for the philosophical discourse that follows.
1.संदर्भः: Traditional practice in Sanskrit texts to begin with an invocation for the successful completion of the work.
1.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Guru vandana (reverence to teacher), mangala (auspiciousness), and establishing the authority of the tradition.

2.सारांशः: Refutation of the inference that establishes falsity/illusion (मिथ्यात्व). Jayatirtha critiques Advaita's position that the world is mithya (false/illusory).
2.प्रयोजनम्: To establish the reality of the world and differences (भेद) against Advaita's doctrine of illusion (माया).
2.पूर्वपक्षः: Advaitins argue that the world is mithya because it is subject to sublation (बाध) like a dream or rope-snake illusion.
2.सिद्धान्तः: The world and differences are real (सत्य), not illusory. What appears real cannot be dismissed as false merely due to changeability.
2.संदर्भः: Critical response to Advaita Vedanta's concept of maya and mithyatva.
2.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Reality of the world, critique of bādha (sublation), establishment of Dvaita realism (तत्त्ववाद).

3.सारांशः: Refutation of the Advaita doctrine of anirvachanīyatva (indefinability), which claims the world is neither real nor unreal.
3.प्रयोजनम्: To reject the logical inconsistency of claiming something is beyond the categories of real and unreal.
3.पूर्वपक्षः: Advaitins claim the world is anirvachanīya - cannot be categorized as either sat or asat.
3.सिद्धान्तः: Everything must be either real or unreal. The claim of a third category is logically impossible.
3.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Law of excluded middle, rejection of anirvachanīyatva, binary nature of existence.

4.सारांशः: Rejection of the definition of avidya (ignorance) as proposed by Advaitins.
4.प्रयोजनम्: To show the incoherence in defining avidya as the cause of world-appearance.
4.पूर्वपक्षः: Advaitins define avidya as indefinable, beginning-less ignorance that creates the illusion of the world.
4.सिद्धान्तः: The definition of avidya is self-contradictory and lacks valid epistemological foundation.
4.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Nature of avidya, critique of its definition, epistemological problems.

5.सारांशः: Rejection of the means of proof (pramāṇa) for establishing avidya.
5.प्रयोजनम्: To demonstrate that there is no valid means of knowledge that can establish the existence of avidya.
5.पूर्वपक्षः: Advaitins claim avidya is known through inference or scripture.
5.सिद्धान्तः: Neither perception, inference, nor scripture validly establishes avidya as defined by Advaitins.
5.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Pramāṇa analysis, validity of knowledge, epistemological critique.

6.सारांशः: Rejection of alternative explanations regarding perceptibility (dṛśyatva).
6.प्रयोजनम्: To refute the argument that perceptibility implies unreality.
6.पूर्वपक्षः: Advaitins argue that whatever is perceivable (dṛśya) is unreal, like objects in a dream.
6.सिद्धान्तः: Perceptibility does not imply unreality; the argument confuses different categories.
6.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Dṛśyatva hetu, dream analogy critique, valid inference rules.

7.सारांशः: Rejection of perceptibility as a reason (hetu) for establishing falsity.
7.प्रयोजनम्: To show that being perceivable cannot be used as logical reason to prove unreality.
7.तार्किकविश्लेषणम्: Analysis of the inference: "World is unreal because it is perceivable." Shows this lacks proper vyāpti.
7.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Hetu-dosha (defect in reason), vyāpti failure, logical rigor.

8.सारांशः: Rejection of insentience (jaḍatva) as a reason for establishing unreality.
8.प्रयोजनम्: To refute the claim that insentient objects are therefore unreal.
8.पूर्वपक्षः: Advaitins argue that jaḍa (insentient) objects like pots are unreal.
8.सिद्धान्तः: Insentience is a real property; it does not imply unreality of the object.
8.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Jaḍatva analysis, reality of material objects, categories of existence.

9.सारांशः: Rejection of finitude/limitation (paricchinnatva) as a reason for unreality.
9.प्रयोजनम्: To establish that limited objects can be real.
9.पूर्वपक्षः: Advaitins claim that anything limited or finite is unreal; only unlimited Brahman is real.
9.सिद्धान्तः: Finitude is a real attribute and does not negate the reality of an entity.
9.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Paricchinnatva, nature of limitation, svatantra-paratantra bheda.

10.सारांशः: How perception (pratyakṣa) contradicts the inference of falsity.
10.प्रयोजनम्: To show that direct perception contradicts the claim that the world is unreal.
10.तार्किकविश्लेषणम्: Pratyakṣa is a stronger pramāṇa than anumāna. When they conflict, perception prevails.
10.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Pratyakṣa-bādha, hierarchy of pramāṇas, primacy of direct perception.

11.सारांशः: How the inference of falsity contradicts scripture (śruti).
11.प्रयोजनम्: To demonstrate that Vedic texts affirm the reality of the world, not its falsity.
11.पूर्वपक्षः: Advaitins interpret śruti passages like "neha nānāsti kiñcana" to mean the world is false.
11.सिद्धान्तः: Proper interpretation of śruti shows it affirms bheda (difference) and reality of the world.
11.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Śruti interpretation, Vedic authority, proper hermeneutics.

12.सारांशः: How the inference of falsity contradicts smrti (traditional texts).
12.प्रयोजनम्: To show that Bhagavad Gītā, Mahābhārata, and other smṛtis affirm reality.
12.सिद्धान्तः: Smṛti texts consistently teach the reality of differences and the world.
12.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Smṛti pramāṇa, Gītā teachings, authority of tradition.

13.सारांशः: How the inference of falsity contradicts other valid inferences.
13.प्रयोजनम्: To demonstrate internal logical contradictions in the Advaita position.
13.तार्किकविश्लेषणम्: Shows that the inference of mithyātva contradicts other accepted inferences in Advaita itself.

14.सारांशः: Examination of the absence of what is to be proved (sādhya) in the example (dṛṣṭānta).
14.प्रयोजनम्: To show that the examples used by Advaitins do not actually prove unreality.
14.तार्किकविश्लेषणम्: Analysis of dṛṣṭānta-dosha. Examples like rope-snake, dream, etc., fail to establish the sādhya.
14.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Dṛṣṭānta validity, sādhya-vikalya, proper analogical reasoning.

15.सारांशः: Defeat of reasons for falsity through counter-reasoning (pratikūla-tarka).
15.प्रयोजनम्: To present positive arguments for reality that overcome arguments for unreality.
15.तार्किकविश्लेषणम्: Use of tarka (reasoning) to show that the same logic used for mithyātva proves satyatva.
15.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Pratikūla-tarka, dialectical reasoning, turning opponent's logic.

16.सारांशः: Sublation/contradiction of the inference regarding partiality (aṃśitva).
16.प्रयोजनम्: To refute the claim that jīva is a part (aṃśa) of Brahman in the Advaita sense.
16.पूर्वपक्षः: Some argue jīva is an aṃśa of Brahman, subject to limitation by avidya.
16.सिद्धान्तः: While jīva is dependent on Brahman, the relationship is not one of avidya-caused partition.

17.सारांशः: Rejection of the inference establishing partiality.
17.प्रयोजनम्: Further refutation of incorrect understanding of jīva-Brahman relationship.
17.सिद्धान्तः: Jīva is eternally distinct from Brahman, not a temporary partition.
17.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Eternal distinction, jīva-svabhāva, rejection of identity.

18.सारांशः: The reasons for falsity are ineffective/useless (aprayojaka).
18.प्रयोजनम्: To show that none of the reasons given for mithyātva actually accomplish their purpose.
18.तार्किकविश्लेषणम्: Systematic examination showing each hetu for mithyātva fails to prove the sādhya.
18.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Aprayojakatva, failure of proof, inadequacy of arguments.

19.सारांशः: Presentation of counter-reasoning for the reasons establishing reality (satyatva).
19.प्रयोजनम्: To positively establish the reality of the world through valid reasoning.
19.सिद्धान्तः: The world is real, as established by perception, scripture, and valid inference.
19.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Positive establishment of reality, satyatva-hetu, valid pramāṇas.

20.सारांशः: Examination of the meaning of the scriptural text "neha nānāsti kiñcana" (There is no diversity here).
20.प्रयोजनम्: To provide correct interpretation of this Upaniṣadic passage against Advaita misinterpretation.
20.पूर्वपक्षः: Advaitins interpret this to mean no real differences exist.
20.सिद्धान्तः: The passage negates seeing diversity in Brahman alone, not all diversity everywhere.
20.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Śruti interpretation, context analysis, correct hermeneutics.

21.सारांशः: Examination of the meaning of "ekam eva" (One alone) scriptural passages.
21.प्रयोजनम्: To explain the Upaniṣadic teaching of oneness without negating real differences.
21.पूर्वपक्षः: Advaitins claim "ekam eva" means only non-dual Brahman exists.
21.सिद्धान्तः: Oneness refers to Brahman's uniqueness and supremacy, not absence of real entities.
21.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Ekatva interpretation, Brahman supremacy, compatible plurality.

22.सारांशः: Meaning of the scripture "satyaṃ jñānam anantam brahma" (Brahman is truth, knowledge, infinite).
22.प्रयोजनम्: To explain Brahman's defining attributes without implying world-unreality.
22.सिद्धान्तः: These are essential attributes of Brahman that distinguish it from dependent entities.

23.सारांशः: Contradiction in the reasoning that appears to sublate difference (bheda).
23.प्रयोजनम्: To refute arguments claiming to sublate or negate real differences.
23.तार्किकविश्लेषणम्: Shows internal contradictions in attempts to prove bheda-bādha.
23.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Bheda-bādha critique, reality of differences, logical analysis.

24.सारांशः: Statement that the inference of falsity of difference is conditional/qualified (sopādhika).
24.प्रयोजनम्: To show the inference has unacknowledged conditions that invalidate it.
24.तार्किकविश्लेषणम्: Identifies hidden upādhis (conditions) in the Advaita argument.

25.सारांशः: The objection (pūrvapakṣa) that difference cannot be grasped by perception and other means.
25.प्रयोजनम्: To present and then refute the claim that bheda is not known through pramāṇas.
25.पूर्वपक्षः: Opponents claim difference is not directly perceived or validly known.
25.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Epistemology of difference, pramāṇa access, preparatory to refutation.

26.सारांशः: Justification that difference is the essential nature (svarūpa) of the substrate (dharmin).
26.प्रयोजनम्: To establish that bheda is intrinsic to objects, not imposed or illusory.
26.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Svarūpa-bheda, intrinsic nature, essence vs. accident.

27.सारांशः: Justification that time and other entities are established through sākṣin (witness-consciousness).
27.प्रयोजनम्: To validate the reality of time and similar categories through inner perception.
27.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Sākṣi-pramāṇa, inner witness, validity of internal perception.

28.प्रयोजनम्: To establish sākṣi as a valid means of knowledge.
28.सिद्धान्तः: Sākṣi is self-validating and directly reveals its objects.

29.सारांशः: Justification of intrinsic validity (svataḥ-prāmāṇya) of knowledge.
29.प्रयोजनम्: To establish that valid knowledge is self-validating, not requiring external proof.
29.सिद्धान्तः: Prāmāṇya is svataḥ (intrinsic), while invalidity requires separate proof.

30.सारांशः: Conclusion of the justification that time is known through sākṣi.
30.प्रयोजनम्: To summarize and conclude the argument for sākṣi-knowledge of time.

31.सारांशः: Rescue from the charge of mutual dependence (anyonya-āśraya) in the perception of difference.
31.प्रयोजनम्: To refute the objection that knowing difference requires prior knowledge of terms, causing circularity.
31.तार्किकविश्लेषणम्: Shows that perception of bheda and terms can be simultaneous, avoiding circularity.
31.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Anyonya-āśraya refutation, simultaneous cognition, logical defense.

32.सारांशः: Demonstration of the simultaneity of cognitions of attribute (dharma), counter-correlate (pratiyogin), and difference (bheda).
32.प्रयोजनम्: To establish that these cognitions occur together, not sequentially.
32.सिद्धान्तः: Dharma, pratiyogin, and bheda are cognized simultaneously in one cognitive act.

33.प्रयोजनम्: To establish the reality of individuating properties that make each entity unique.
33.पूर्वपक्षः: Opponents deny real viśeṣas, claiming differences are conventional or unreal.
33.सिद्धान्तः: Viśeṣas are real and necessary to account for individuation and difference.

34.सारांशः: Conclusion of the rejection of objections to the cognition of difference.
34.प्रयोजनम्: To summarize all refutations of challenges to bheda-pratyaya (cognition of difference).
34.सिद्धान्तः: Bheda is validly cognized through perception and other pramāṇas; all objections fail.
34.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Summary of epistemological defense, validity of bheda-knowledge, comprehensive refutation.

35.सारांशः: Contradiction of the thesis (pratijñā) in the inference of falsity of difference.
35.प्रयोजनम्: To show that the very statement of the thesis contradicts itself.
35.तार्किकविश्लेषणम्: Demonstrates pratijñā-virodha: asserting "bheda is false" requires acknowledging difference.

36.सारांशः: Statement of deviation (vyabhicāra) and other defects in the inference that tries to establish the falsity of difference (bheda).
36.प्रयोजनम्: Final refutation of Advaita's claim that differences are false, establishing the permanent reality of पञ्चभेद.
36.सिद्धान्तः: The five-fold difference (पञ्चभेद) is eternally real and cannot be sublated or shown to be false.
36.मुख्यबिन्दवः: Vyabhicāra (deviation in inference), reality of bheda (difference), finality of Dvaita position.

_glossary.मिथ्यात्व: Falsity, illusion - the Advaita doctrine that the world is neither real nor unreal
_glossary.अनिर्वाच्यत्व: Indefinability - claim that the world cannot be categorized as real or unreal
_glossary.अविद्या: Ignorance - in Advaita, the inexplicable power that creates world-appearance
_glossary.दृश्यत्व: Perceptibility - quality of being an object of perception
_glossary.परिच्छिन्नत्व: Finitude, limitation - quality of being bounded
_glossary.व्याप्ति: Universal concomitance - invariable relation between hetu (reason) and sādhya (conclusion)
_glossary.अनुमान: Inference - one of the valid means of knowledge (pramāṇa)
_glossary.हेतु: Reason, logical mark - the middle term in an inference
_glossary.दृष्टान्त: Example, instance - supporting case in an inference
_glossary.साध्य: What is to be proved - the conclusion in an inference
_glossary.बाध: Sublation - cancellation of one knowledge by another (used by Advaitins)
_glossary.भेद: Difference - the fundamental reality in Dvaita philosophy
_glossary.पञ्चभेद: Five-fold difference - between jīva-jīva, jīva-Īśvara, jīva-jada, jada-jada, Īśvara-jada
_glossary.तत्त्ववाद: Realism - Dvaita's position that differences and the world are ultimately real
_glossary.व्यभिचार: Deviation - logical fallacy where the reason is not invariably connected to the conclusion
_glossary.प्रत्यक्ष: Perception - direct sensory knowledge, primary pramāṇa
_glossary.श्रुति: Revealed scripture - the Vedas and Upaniṣads
_glossary.अंशित्व: Partiality - being a part or portion of something
_glossary.तर्क: Reasoning, dialectics - supportive reasoning in philosophical argument
_glossary.धर्मिन्: Substrate, subject - that which possesses attributes
_glossary.प्रामाण्य: Validity of knowledge - quality of being a valid cognition
_glossary.अन्योन्याश्रय: Mutual dependence, circularity - logical fallacy of circular reasoning
_glossary.प्रतियोगिन्: Counter-correlate - the second term in a relation
_glossary.विशेष: Individuating attribute, particular - that which makes an entity unique
_glossary.प्रतिज्ञा: Thesis, proposition - the statement to be proved

_meta.author: Sri Jayatirtha (Teeka Acharya)
_meta.primary_purpose: Refutation of Advaita's mithyatva doctrine and establishment of bheda-vāda
_meta.structure: Follows systematic refutation of Advaita positions on unreality and difference
